method: For every vector carrying an input object plus an expected canonical form (expected_jcs_bytes_b64 / canonical_bytes_utf8 / payload_canonical_json) and/or an expected SHA-256 (canonical_sha256 / *content_sha256 / *_hash), AgentGraph's rfc8785 0.1.4 canonicalizer was run on the input and compared byte-for-byte and digest-for-digest. Adversarial reject vectors are scored on their authoritative expected_* fields; deliberately-stale audit-chain linkage fields (content_hash/prev_hash) are excluded as they are integrity metadata, not canonicalization targets.
scope_note: Covers all 24 anchor-set files in the corpus that contain JCS re-canonicalization vectors (CTEF/APS, action_ref family, AP2 open-mandate-hash, settlement/cancellation/refund/compliance receipts, privacy-class, per-chain envelopes, PEF, ZKP receipt, retention chains, adversarial isolation, multichain ed25519 substrate). The RFC 9421 HTTP-message-signature proxy-chain wire fixtures are out of scope (AgentGraph does not implement the RFC 9421 signing-base layer); AgentGraph shares only the JCS RFC 8785 canonicalization substrate, which is what this attestation covers.
